evals.name: feature-authentication
evals.prompt: Write a commit message for the JWT authentication feature. Ticket DF-438.
expectations: Commit type is 'feat' with scope 'auth'
Header follows conventional commit format
Body explains why JWT was chosen (scalability, stateless, security)
Includes 'Refs: DF-438' footer
Focuses on reasoning and benefits, not just what changed
Mentions technical details about implementation (HS256, configuration)

evals.name: bug-fix-parsing
evals.prompt: Help me write a commit message for fixing the array parsing bug that crashed on multiple spaces.
setup_commands:
echo 'import re

def parse_array(s):
    # Fixed: was using split() which handles any whitespace
    # Now preserves original spacing for accurate reconstruction
    return re.split(r"\s+", s)' > parser.py
expectations: Commit type is 'fix' with scope 'parser'
Header summarizes the specific bug (multiple spaces handling)
Body explains root cause (index mismatch, whitespace handling)
Mentions test coverage addition for regression prevention
Technical details about the fix approach (regex pattern)
Explains impact (crashes, data corruption)

evals.name: commit-review
evals.prompt: Review this commit message and improve it: 'Updated API docs'
expectations: Identifies missing context (what docs were updated, why)
Suggests specific improvements to structure and content
Proposes adding technical details about API changes
Recommends conventional commit format with proper type
Focuses on value to readers and maintainability
Provides concrete example of improved message

evals.name: missing-ticket
evals.prompt: Write a commit message for adding utility functions
expectations: Skill asks for ticket reference before proceeding
Does not generate commit message without ticket info
Provides clear question about ticket identifier
Maintains professional and helpful tone
Explains why ticket reference is needed
